title: acer subglaucum var. rugelii
intro: Acer subglaucum var. rugelii, also known as the rough-barked maple, is a deciduous tree or shrub belonging to the Sapindaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Acer subglaucum var. rugelii have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark of the tree is typically dried and processed into powders, capsules, or teas, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
